ocr.text: In the Ørsted Fiscal Year 2024 report on wind farm capacity factors, the table presents comprehensive data. The first entry,
FarmA, features a total capacity of 150 MW, a capacity factor of 42.5%, revenue of $12M, operating expenses (OPEX) of $3M,
a net income of $1.5M, capital expenditures (CAPEX) of $15M, and is located in Texas, with performance metrics indicating its
environmental impact. Following this, FarmB has a total capacity of 200 MW, a capacity factor of 38.0%, revenue of $15M,
OPEX of $4M, a net income of $2M, CAPEX of $20M, and is situated in California. FarmC records a total capacity of 100
MW, a capacity factor of 45.0%, revenue of $8M, OPEX of $2.5M, net income of $1M, and CAPEX of $10M, located in
Oregon.

$1M  $10M  Oregon  FarmD  250  40.0  $20M  $5M  $3M  $25M  Nevada  FarmE
$1.8M  $18M  Florida  FarmG  220  39.5  $25M  $5.5M  $2.5M  $30M  NewYork  Total
FarmD shows a total capacity of 250 MW, a capacity factor of 40.0%, revenue of $20M, OPEX of $5M, net income of $3M,
and CAPEX of $25M, located in Nevada. FarmE has a total capacity of 300 MW, a capacity factor of 37.5%, revenue of $30M,
OPEX of $6M, net income of $4M, and CAPEX of $35M, situated in Illinois. FarmF, with a total capacity of 180 MW, offers a
capacity factor of 41.0%, revenue of $14M, OPEX of $3.5M, net income of $1.8M, and CAPEX of $18M, is located in Florida.
Lastly, FarmG has a total capacity of 220 MW, a capacity factor of 39.5%, revenue of $25M, OPEX of $5.5M, net income of
$2.5M, and CAPEX of $30M, situated in New York. The total across all farms reveals a combined total capacity of 1,400 MW,
an average capacity factor of 40.0%, total revenue of $110M, total OPEX of $29M, total net income of $14.8M, and total
CAPEX of $153M, summarizing the performance metrics and highlighting the significant contributions of each wind farm to
the renewable energy landscape in North America.
